summary: Solid audience recording of a cover‑heavy show with standout “Big Boss Man” and “Althea”, but an uneven second set leaves the performance mixed.
review: The Oade audience tape captures a relaxed, cover‑laden first set that many reviewers describe as tight and enjoyable, highlighted by a stellar rendition of Big Boss Man and a solid Althea. While the sound quality is praised as clear and “top‑notch,” several listeners note that the second set drags and Jerry’s vocals become fatigued, giving the overall show a mixed feel.

ai_rating.rationale: Most reviewers give high marks for the recording and first‑set performance (70% weight), but several low‑star reviews criticize the second set and vocal fatigue, pulling the overall rating down.

recording_quality.technical_notes: Audience (Oade) tape is praised for clarity and crisp capture of the many cover songs; no major drop‑outs reported, though some listeners note typical ambient crowd noise.

show_quality.setlist_flow: First set is energetic and cover‑heavy, described as ‘tight’ and ‘relaxing.’ The second set is seen by several reviewers as lagging, dragging the overall impression down.

band_member_comments.Jerry: Vocals strong early (toast) but become fatigued/gasping later; mixed praise for his performance on Big Boss Man.
band_member_comments.Phil: Lesh’s vocal mic work noted as uneven (‘all over the place’) during the tour, especially on Mississippi Half‑Step.
